name: Castor oil
description: Castor oil is a type of vegetable oil extracted from the seeds of the castor oil plant (Ricinus communis) that is mainly cultivated in India, South Amercia, Africa and China. It is one of the oldest cultivated crops used for commercial purposes such as manufacturing of personal care products or as a renewable resource in the chemical industry. Castor oil a rich source of ricinoleic acid (RA), where the contents may consist of up to 90% ricinoleic, 4% linoleic, 3% oleic, 1% stearic, and less than 1% linolenic fatty acids[A31170]. By the FDA, castor oil is a food additive permitted for direct addition to food for human consumption. It can be found in hard candies as a release agent and antisticking agent, or in vitamin and mineral tablets as a component of protective coatings.
